summary: Close M2-R09 with a production Workbench Chromium journey for automatic SVG presentation, real wheel zoom, pointer pan, exact fit restoration, and incompatible-mode recovery; the journey exposed and fixed a zero-height SVG row and passive-wheel console error.
specSections: Section 2.7 test-first evidence and milestone gates
FR-3.1 SVG pan, zoom, fit, and dimensions
FR-3.2 automatic and pinned viewer modes

decisions.topic: Viewer grid rows with and without the 3D toolbar
decisions.decision: Use a two-row heading/content grid for empty and 2D viewers, and add the auto-sized middle toolbar row only when 3D geometry actually renders the ViewerToolbar.
decisions.rationale: The unconditional three-row grid placed 2D content in the toolbar's auto row and left the flexible row empty, producing a zero-height SVG interaction canvas despite a large viewer panel.

decisions.decision: Attach the SVG wheel handler directly with passive:false and remove React's passive delegated onWheel binding; retain stateful cursor-centered zoom in the same component.
decisions.rationale: The production browser reported that preventDefault was illegal inside the passive listener. A scoped non-passive listener prevents page scrolling and eliminates the error without changing zoom math.

testEvidence.criterion: Production SVG interaction surface has real layout
testEvidence.test: The dedicated Chromium journey loads the production Workbench/runtime/engine fixture and requires a visible 2D canvas before zoom, pan, fit, and mode actions.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED: 1 failed. The SVG button resolved but was hidden. Browser measurement showed viewer-panel height 638 px, viewer-content height 32 px, and svg-viewer height 0 px because content occupied the unconditional auto toolbar row.

green.outcome: The grid correction made the real SVG viewport visible and allowed every interaction assertion to execute; the widened run then exposed the separate passive-wheel console error recorded below.

testEvidence.criterion: Zoom, pan, fit, and mode interaction is error-free
testEvidence.test: Use real Playwright mouse wheel and drag input, assert scale/transform changes, exact fit restoration, pinned-3D mismatch without stale SVG, Auto recovery, and empty page/console error arrays.

red.outcome: ASSERTION-LEVEL RED after layout repair: all interaction behavior completed, but the final console assertion received 'Unable to preventDefault inside passive event listener invocation.' instead of an empty array.

green.outcome: GREEN: Running 1 test using 1 worker; 1 passed (2.3s). Zoom, pan, fit, pinned mismatch, Auto recovery, and zero page/console errors all passed without retries.

testEvidence.criterion: Existing SVG behavior and static policy remain green
testEvidence.test: Run the focused SvgViewer component tests, complete typecheck, and lint/source-policy gates after replacing the wheel binding and viewer grid class.

red.command: Covered by the two real-browser assertion-level failures above; no test or policy was weakened.
red.outcome: The pre-change production surface was both non-interactive at zero height and console-red under wheel input.

green.command: pnpm.cmd exec vitest run tests/ui/viewer/SvgViewer.test.tsx --reporter=verbose; pnpm.cmd typecheck; pnpm.cmd lint
green.outcome: GREEN: SvgViewer Test Files 1 passed (1), Tests 3 passed (3), Duration 884ms; TypeScript exited 0; Biome checked 274 files with no fixes; source policy passed with UI files within 400 lines and platform boundaries intact.

questions: M2-R09 is closed by this retained production-route Chromium interaction journey and the two resulting product corrections.
No owner similarity gate, comparison repository, workflow content, or prohibited source was opened or executed.
attestation: No prohibited source was consulted in producing this change.
